name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208d98b1dd8c07cb053faac776909896d5c11378d8d95d049b48b5d9bb970d310fe61c5a8017e970742ef3ec7d9defe65d81
White House Counsel reveals the secret of Trump’s judicial selection process

- The Daily Caller

White House Counsel Don McGahn joined the Conservative Political Action Conference this week, where he detailed President Donald J. Trump’s judicial selection process. McGahn noted that selecting judges has been an integral part of the President’s deregulatory agenda, and that the President is “particularly keen on nominees who will police federal agencies.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208d78fef27888a307cf7bf70b569a75a40a298a6d75277b2936baba172bc1b9b42ef7100879babff044c4c5b78442490caf

7 times Trump has been tougher on Russia than Obama

- The Washington Free Beacon

Elizabeth Harrington at The Washington Free Beacon details 7 ways in which President Trump has been tougher on Russia than President Obama. Her list includes missile defense, arming Ukraine, closing Russian diplomatic facilities, and disavowing the Iran Deal.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208d5e8f92369e14b5e632262b43ec8261c3aa9c92b6d0b2c9e2353c529fa14b95489594c6d077688738febeb37955e1f204
U.S. Treasury official says era of congratulating China is over

- Bloomberg

Andrew Mayeda reports that a senior U.S. Treasury official says America wants to see China “back up its professed support for open trade” instead of “perpetuating a system that worked for their benefit but ended up costing jobs in most of the rest of the world.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208d1d5f2d78455fbdd98778313d4d5ff3dbfeefe2d3b8d1c186e55961043f4d7bd74a1bb3bc4097e6e300ed8016a55404de

White House says US could reach 3 percent growth rate

- The Associated Press

The Council of Economic Advisers released its annual economic report this week, which predicts average annual growth of 2.2 percent through 2028. The report also notes that if there is a “full implementation of the Administration’s agenda,” the U.S. economy would likely reach a growth rate of 3 percent through the next decade.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208d236f6a730d1487345e7633b830edd896b93d7b4e307b7c6c2464dfeabb66e58f908cae9a11f5543b4d8a7077425328e3
Bernie blames Hillary for allowing Russian interference

- Politico

Politico documents Democratic infighting on the question of Russia. “The real question to be asked is what was the Clinton campaign [doing about Russian interference]? They had more information about this than we did,” Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-VT) asked on Wednesday.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208da2e564259a72747a985bd1c10e8a86c239e0c959f60684eb5b362f75a7201d68850faf338a398fe059e4699b727fad40
Don't forget small businesses. We're booming under Trump tax cuts, too

- USA Today

In an op-ed, Gary Rabine writes about the tremendous effects of the President’s tax cuts on small businesses. He notes that his company alone is “green-lighting two start-ups, doubling research and development, hiring more people and giving larger raises and bonuses.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208d4eb82fa0b0fb4c0e49a59b37def9772c68be0362564abcab8fc7a5b54e9eb5a6e706444eee27433d6f60a4fa3efc3afc
Ivanka Trump to meet South Korea's Moon on Olympics trip: official

- Reuters

Ivanka Trump is headed to South Korea to lead the U.S. delegation to the closing ceremony of the 2018 Winter Olympics. She will reaffirm the U.S.-South Korea alliance and cheer on the great American athletes who represent our country on the world stage.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208d93043aa899e16f1322e974cbfc7cbce9a8b4c84763945e09b37af2b1888c917a2da85b86975c6aeeee81af106dde0a87
Black and Decker expansion to bring 400 jobs to Baltimore County

- WMAR Baltimore

On Tuesday, the company Stanley Black and Decker announced its plans to expand operations in Maryland. The company plans to hire for 400 positions across electrical and electromechanical engineering, digital and brand marketing, industrial design, finance and human resources.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208d9e52bb8e9d81c67a9d7ad2ff19e39c5bc094adc26545a4a071b1a4671242200c6e7c3cb3b8109b34de95999e76aee877
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fd36067b0d21208dc9d9b6a02084f30fb85649dcedbd5518125f15e81d44270c429f200ae47cd795137c38acfb4156f69e073ce45eee2ce7
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d465e4f0cc0248caecb5e592b03721a118bd2c5e345d96fabb55f93d2867cabd3f2daf33db48d78632c3cb1d2582f89933468c509ad4728a62
Unsubscribe

body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Hey there,

I've made it a personal mission to fight for a country where all opinions are heard and respected.

With Republicans gerrymandering districts to serve their own interests, they're corroding our democracy. _They 're minimizing our voices._

We do it because **Joe 's race is consistently listed as one of the top 5 Senate races in the country -- in fact, _Politico_ ranked Indiana's seat the 3rd most likely to flip parties**. name: Ben Jealous
subject: Youth power > NRA
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friend -
This week, in a watershed moment, thousands of young people in Florida marched to their state capital to demand gun reform. In solidarity, thousands of Maryland students marched on the U.S. Capitol demanding stricter gun safety laws, getting weapons of war out of the hands of those who would do harm, and long-term solutions from our legislators.
Just a day later, the head of the NRA was in Maryland, coarsely accusing those that want to do something about gun violence in our country as agents of socialism, and speaking out against common-sense bipartisan gun control.
The young people who marched all across our country to demand action continue to show more courage than politicians like Larry Hogan, **who opposes a ban on bump stocks and refuses to tell us what he promised the NRA to get their endorsement.**
[**If you agree that we need leaders who will put our young people first, donate $5 to our campaign today.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a721/7ab0e2c4/7f474039/349a14b2/3213252295/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst2-18?refcode=OHQ02248B")
I've got two kids in Montgomery County public schools. Just days ago, another individual was caught bringing a gun to school. What does it say to my kids when some politicians won't take responsibility for their safety or demand accountability when the system fails them?
The system is broken, but young people are pushing back. As we bear witness to a painful but unique moment in our nation's history, where young people have stepped up to become leaders and arbiters of change, we have a responsibility to step up on their behalf to make this new year safer than years past.
To Larry Hogan: Last week's heartbreak in Florida is yet another in a long line of failures from our leaders putting NRA donations above our children's safety, and that is unacceptable. We must remain accountable to the individuals we serve.
To our young people: we know that we must organize a progressive wave of change to move our country forward. You are a critical part of that, and now it's time to make your voice heard. So volunteer or vote people into office who share your beliefs, who will fight for you, and will continually affirm and empower you.
Remember: we elect our legislators to make it easier for you to begin implementing change in your community and this country.
[**If you believe in the power of this campaign to make it easier for you make change, chip in $5 to us today.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a721/7ab0e2c4/7f474039/349a14b2/3213252295/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst2-18?refcode=OHQ02248B")
Florida has to be the last. Say it with me: never again.
Onward,
Ben

**[DONATE](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a721/7ab0e2c4/7f474039/349a14b2/3213252295/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst2-18?refcode=OHQ02248B")**

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER

Email is one of the most critical tools for our campaign, but you can let us know if you would like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a721/7ab0e2c4/7f474039/349a14bc/3213252295/VEsD/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=emfooter") going forward. body:
Friends -

This is about the time that I would normally tell you about our end-of-month fundraising goal and ask for your help to meet it. But this isn't a normal month, and I know that you don't need a number to get motivated.

Last week, Donald Trump stepped directly into this race by endorsing an anti-immigrant extremist who enthusiastically voted for the GOP's anti-middle-class tax scheme. We don't need another Republican in Washington who makes life harder for working folks by putting corporate interests ahead of the middle class. And we certainly can't afford to let Republicans flip this seat and deepen their Senate majority.

So no, this isn't a normal email, because this isn't a normal election. I know that you feel as strongly as I do about protecting the middle class and that, come November, you will hold Republicans responsible for the mess they've created with their corporate giveaways.

**If you have $5 or more to invest in this race, it will go a long way towards preserving Democrats ' chances of taking back the Senate. **

**Here 's the best link: _[https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6a3e/51dbcc2a/abcff20/3a301d8/668395799/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDIyNF9mcl9uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180224_fr_nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Bob
